channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: OpenAI Launches Free Training Course for Teachers to Master AI and Prompt Engineering
item.description: OpenAI partners with Common Sense Media to provide teachers with a free training course on artificial intelligence, prompt engineering, and responsible AI use in education.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Uber announces ‘XXL’ rides. Here’s what to know about the new service
item.description: Uber Technologies launched “XXL” rides with extra trunk space on Wednesday, betting on increased demand stemming from Thanksgiving holiday travelers, as the ride-hailing company seeks to overcome a slowdown in its mainstay app-based taxi business.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Iggy Azalea explains why she’s gone all in on crypto
item.description: Rapper and entrepreneur Iggy Azalea’s crypto token $MOTHER isn’t just another “pump and dump” celebrity crypto play—it’s her next act.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: It's not just free VPNs, these premium services may be bad for your privacy
item.description: New research unveils the risks of paid Android VPNs, from data leaks to weaker encryption and dubious data sharing. Here's all you need to know.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Chatbot's disturbing message
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Network connectivity: An urgent matter of national security
item.description: Building a network architecture and organizational structure that can respond dynamically to new challenges and technologies is critical. The post Network connectivity: An urgent matter of national security first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: US gathers allies to talk AI safety. Trump's vow to undo Biden's AI policy overshadows their work
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has vowed to repeal President Joe Biden's signature artificial intelligence policy when he returns to the White House for a second term. What that actually means for the future of AI technology remains to be seen.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Microsoft Ignite 2024 — get the latest news and insights
item.description: Microsoft Ignite 2024 kicks off in Chicago and runs Nov. 19-22. If you can’t make it to Chicago, no worries. First, the physical event is sold out, according to the Ignite event page. Second, it’s a hybrid event, so you can attend Ignite virtually.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: San Jose office building linked to high-profile firm flops into default
item.description: A big San Jose office building whose principal owner is a high-profile real estate firm has tumbled into a loan default
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Supply chain threats highlight security gaps in LLMs and AI
item.description: Open-source AI faces increasing security threats; learn how LLM vulnerabilities impact software supply chains.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Want to launch a super app? Here's what you need to know
item.description: Thomas Kriebernegg, General Manager, SplitMetrics Agency, shares his top tips on how to launch a super app.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: OpenTelemetry certification launches
item.description: A new certification program from the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) and Linux Foundation is aimed at validating the skills needed to utilize OpenTelemetry to gain visibility across distributed systems.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: GSA listening more to customers to improve IAE platforms
item.description: Memi Whitehead, the deputy assistant commissioner for GSA’s Integrated Award Environment, said new tools aim to improve its users’ experiences. The post GSA listening more to customers to improve IAE platforms first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: NetOps startup Selector AI wants to solve the network noise problem
item.description: Back in 2019, Kannan Kothandaraman helped to start Selector AI after spending years in the networking trenches at big vendors including Juniper and Cisco.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Cable TV Giant Comcast Spins Off Major Networks Amid Streaming Shift
item.description: Comcast spins off cable TV networks, shifts focus towards streaming services
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Siete formas de implementar su propio LLM
item.description: La inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa es la nueva tecnología que más rápido se está desarrollando en la historia. Está transformando el mundo y, según la Encuesta de población en tiempo real a más de 3.000 adultos que trabajan en EE.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Australia won't force social media users to share their personal details when child ban takes effect
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia's communications minister said Wednesday the government won't force social media users to hand over their personal information to tech companies, as children younger than 16 are set to be banned from the platforms.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: La IA generativa redefine la educación secundaria; así es GenAI4ED
item.description: El pasado mes de octubre arrancaba el proyecto GenAI4ED, una iniciativa internacional financiada a cargo de Horizonte Europa que cuenta con la participación de IMDEA Networks.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Meta creates ‘Business AI’ group led by ex-Salesforce AI CEO Clara Shih
item.description: Meta has hired the former CEO of Salesforce AI, Clara Shih, to lead a new “Business AI” group. Salesforce has still not publicly acknowledged Shih’s departure, but it will be hard to ignore now that she’s announced her new role on her LinkedIn profile.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Why the crypto community loves Howard Lutnick, Trump’s pick for Commerce secretary
item.description: Donald Trump’s selection on Tuesday of Howard Lutnick as Commerce secretary brought cheers from the cryptocurrency sector. Little surprise there. Lutnick has long advocated for cryptocurrency to be treated on equal footing with other financial assets.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft unveils imaging APIs for Windows Copilot Runtime
item.description: Microsoft’s Windows Copilot Runtime, which allows developers to integrate AI capabilities into Windows, is being fitted with AI-backed APIs for image processing. It will also gain access to Phi 3.5 Silica, a custom-built generative AI model for Copilot+ PCs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The dark energy pushing our universe apart may not be what it seems, scientists say
item.description: Distant, ancient galaxies are giving scientists more hints that a mysterious force called dark energy may not be what they thought.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Amazon and SpaceX are challenging the NLRB’s constitutionality in federal court
item.description: Attorneys for Amazon and Elon Musk’s SpaceX argued in a federal appeals court Monday that the National Labor Relations Board’s structure is unconstitutional, advancing a legal fight that may last into the Trump administration where Musk is expected to oversee bureaucratic cost-cutting.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: New eagle camera is going live in Minnesota to the delight of global viewers
item.description: Eagle lovers around the world who were crushed when the nest of a Minnesota pair and their chick collapsed on live video last year will get a when a new eagle camera goes live this week.
item.dc:creator: Steve Karnowski, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Government commission proposes Manhattan Project-style AI funding plan
item.description: A U.S. congressional commission proposed a "Manhattan Project-like" initiative for the funding of artificial intelligence (AI) development in a larger push to stay ahead of China's technological advancements. The report, issued Tuesday by the bipartisan U.S.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft pitches AI 'agents' that can perform tasks on their own at Ignite 2024
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella told customers at a conference in Chicago on Tuesday that the company is teaching a new set of artificial intelligence tools how to "act on our behalf across our work and life.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Forget driverless cars. One company wants autonomous helicopters to spray crops and fight fires
item.description: HENNIKER, N.H. (AP) — When Hector Xu was learning to fly a helicopter in college, he recalled having a few “nasty experiences” while trying to navigate at night.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Instagram rolls out option to reset recommendations
item.description: Instagram is testing a new feature that allows users to reset their content recommendations on the platform when in need of a "fresh start.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Nvidia's Blackwell Data Center Processors Face Overheating Issues, Redesigns Required
item.description: Nvidia's next-generation Blackwell data center processors face overheating issues, forcing redesigns of server racks and potentially delaying shipments to major customers like Google, Meta, and Microsoft.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Company affiliated with Alex Jones seeks to disqualify The Onion's auction bid on Infowars
item.description: A company affiliated with conspiracy theorist Alex Jones asked a federal judge on Monday to disqualify a bid by the satirical news outlet The Onion to buy Jones' Infowars at a bankruptcy auction, alleging fraud and collusion.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: IBM announces 50-fold quantum speed improvement
item.description: IBM launched its most advanced quantum computer yet last week at its inaugural quantum developer conference. It features nearly twice the gates of last year’s quantum utility demonstration – and a 50-fold speed increase.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Windows 10 Insider Previews: A guide to the builds
item.description: Microsoft never sleeps. In addition to its steady releases of major and minor updates to the current version of Windows 10, the company frequently rolls out public preview builds to members of its Windows Insider Program, allowing them to test out — and even help shape — upcoming features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New Chinese humanoid robot shows off its strength by lifting 35 pounds per hand
item.description: China's Shanghai Kepler Robotics has developed a new humanoid robot that can carry up to 35 pounds per hand for commercial applications across various industries
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: One Tech Tip: Replacing passwords with passkeys for an easier login experience
item.description: LONDON (AP) — If you're tired of memorizing passwords, then give passkeys a try. You might have noticed that many online services are now offering the option of using passkeys, a digital authentication method touted as an easier and more secure way to log in.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Elon Musk says he and Trump have 'mandate to delete' regulations. Ethics laws could limit Musk role
item.description: In picking billionaire Elon Musk to be “our cost cutter” for the U.S. government, President-elect Donald Trump won't be the first American president to empower a business tycoon to look for ways to dramatically cut federal regulations. President Ronald Reagan tapped J.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, ABC 4
